content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     About us     Shareholder Centre     Alternative securities   Alternative securities   View NAB's alternative securities that include Capital Notes, Subordinated Notes, Preference Shares and American Depository Receipts.    NAB Capital Notes 8    NAB Capital Notes 8 are convertible notes directly issued by NAB, and listed on the ASX under the code NABPK.     More about NAB Capital Notes 8    NAB Capital Notes 7    NAB Capital Notes 7 are convertible notes directly issued by NAB, and listed on the ASX under the code NABPJ.     More about the NAB Capital Notes 7    NAB Capital Notes 6    NAB Capital Notes 6 are convertible notes directly issued by NAB, and listed on the ASX under the code NABPI.     More about the NAB Capital Notes 6    NAB Capital Notes 5    NAB Capital Notes 5 are convertible notes directly issued by NAB, and listed on the ASX under the code NABPH.     More about the NAB Capital Notes 5    NAB Capital Notes 4    On Thursday, 12 March 2020, NAB announced that it is withdrawing its offer of NAB Capital Notes 4.     More about the withdrawal of NAB Capital Notes 4, opens in new window    NAB Capital Notes 3    NAB Capital Notes 3 are convertible notes directly issued by NAB, and listed on the ASX under the code NABPF.     More about the NAB Capital Notes 3    NAB Subordinated Notes 2    NAB Subordinated Notes 2 are direct, subordinated, unsecured obligations of NAB, and were formerly listed on the ASX under the code NABPE.     More about the NAB Subordinated Notes 2    NAB Capital Notes 2    NAB Capital Notes 2, formerly listed on the ASX under the code NABPD, were redeemed on 7 July 2022.     More about the NAB Capital Notes 2    NAB Capital Notes    NAB Capital Notes, formerly listed on the ASX under code NABPC, were resold on 23 March 2020.     More about the NAB Capital Notes    NAB Convertible Preference Shares II    NAB Convertible Preference Shares II were fully paid preference shares issued directly by NAB.     More about the Convertible Preference Shares II    NAB Convertible Preference Shares    NAB Convertible Preference Shares were fully paid preference shares issued directly by NAB.     More about the Convertible Preference Shares    NAB Subordinated Notes    NAB Subordinated Notes, formerly listed on the ASX under code NABHB, were redeemed on their first optional redemption date of Monday, 19 June 2017.     More about NAB Subordinated Notes    National Income Securities    National Income Securities were a form of interest income security. They were not ordinary shares or bank deposits, and were formerly listed on the ASX under code NABHA. National Income Securities were repaid on 15 February 2021.     More about National Income Securities    American Depositary Receipt (ADR) program    The National Australia Bank Limited (NAB) American Depositary Receipt (ADR) program is managed by Deutsche Bank. It is a level 1 sponsored program that operates on the OTC Pink trading platform.     More about American Depositary Receipts    Shareholder Centre     Dividend information    Alternative securities    Hybrid Securities Education    Financial calendar    Financial disclosures and reporting    ASX announcements    Annual General Meeting    Regulatory disclosures    Share register profile    Share Purchase Plan (2020)    CYBG Demerger    Retail Entitlement Offer   Important information    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.
